item.title: Principal’s Shah Says Tech Is Haven Trade in a ‘Fragile’ Market
item.description: Big tech stocks are proving to be a safe bet for equity investors looking for a harbor in a “fragile” market brought about by the Iran war, according to Seema Shah at Principal Asset Management.

item.title: North Sea Oil Fetches Discount for First Time During Iran War
item.description: A North Sea oil grade that’s central to setting the world’s most important physical crude price traded at a discount for the first time during the Iran war, as immediate fears ease of a supply shock in Europe.

item.title: US 10-Year Treasury Yield Hits Highest Since July After PPI Data
item.description: The yield on benchmark 10-year Treasuries rose to the highest since July after US wholesale inflation accelerated in April to the fastest pace since 2022.

item.title: Iran War Tests Turkish Central Bank’s Ambitious Inflation Target
item.description: A global energy price shock sparked by the US-Israeli war on Iran is forcing Turkey’s central bank to raise its inflation forecasts, putting further strain on the country’s efforts to slow price gains.

item.title: US Producer Prices Rise Most Since 2022 on Energy Costs
item.description: US wholesale inflation accelerated in April to the fastest pace since 2022 on a war-driven increase in energy prices that’s feeding into higher freight transportation costs.

item.title: Czechs Say Monetary Policy Still Tight After Jump in Inflation
item.description: Czech monetary policy is still restrictive even after an acceleration in inflation due to the fallout of the Iran war, although price risks require more caution, according to the central bank.

item.title: Swiss Population Cap Study Shows 12% GDP Hit by End of Century
item.description: A Swiss population cap of 10 million risks curbing economic output by as much as 12% through the end of the century, according to a government-commissioned study.

item.title: Stock Futures Signal Rebound as Dip Buyers Snap Up Tech Shares
item.description: US equity futures pushed higher early Wednesday as traders snapped up technology shares after a pullback in the group, with enthusiasm around strong earnings outweighing a resurgence in inflation.

item.title: Nvidia, Tesla Shares Advance as CEOs Join Trump’s Trip to China
item.description: An upcoming meeting between US President Donald Trump and his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping could be the next test for stocks that are clocking one record after the next.

item.title: Morgan Stanley Hikes S&P 500 Target to 8,300 on Earnings Boom
item.description: Morgan Stanley is turning more positive on US stocks in a bet that blockbuster earnings and a strong economy will keep the bull market running full steam ahead.

item.title: Saudis Tell OPEC That Oil Output Sank Again to Lowest Since 1990
item.description: Saudi Arabia reported to OPEC that its crude oil production collapsed further last month to the lowest since 1990, as the Iran war choked off exports from the Persian Gulf.

item.title: Insider One Buys Bluecore in AI Marketing Drive Ahead of IPO
item.description: Istanbul-based startup Insider One bought Bluecore Inc to expand its US reach in artificial intelligence marketing ahead of a planned initial public offering.

item.title: Deutsche Bank, Santander Test World Bank’s Risk Transfer Engine
item.description: Deutsche Bank AG and Banco Santander SA are among financial institutions participating in a milestone synthetic securitization designed to help the World Bank Group leverage its ability to provide loans to emerging-market borrowers.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for May 13, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures rise 0.2% as of 7:49 a.m. in New York ahead of a summit between President Donald Trump and China’s Xi Jinping.

item.title: AI Hyperscalers Mull Going Deeper Into Next-Gen Nuclear Supply Chain
item.description: Tech companies are considering investments in the fuel for small modular reactors.

item.title: Holed-Up Philippine Senator Courts Support Over Police Standoff
item.description: Two days after he dramatically re-emerged into public view, a Philippine politician with an outstanding arrest warrant is holed up in the Senate with police massed outside, one of the more bizarre episodes in the country’s politics in recent years.

item.title: Poland Stands By Digital Services Tax Plan, Rebuffing US Threats
item.description: Poland will continue working on legislation to tax digital platforms, Finance Minister Andrzej Domanski said, despite threats of retaliation from the US administration of Donald Trump.

item.title: Nebius Reports 684% Sales Jump On AI Data Center Growth
item.description: Cloud computing provider Nebius Group NV reported a 684% jump in first quarter sales on increased demand for its data centers.

item.title: Samsung and SK Hynix Still Look Like Bargains Compared to Tech Peers
item.description: South Korea’s top two companies have enthralled the nation but may still be underestimated

item.title: Iran’s Kharg Island Oil Jetties Empty Again Yesterday, Satellite Shows
item.description: The oil jetties at Iran’s Kharg Island were once again empty on Tuesday, curbing the nation’s ability to export crude, satellite images collected by Bloomberg show.

item.title: Trump on US Banknotes? A Social Media Post Spurs Speculation
item.description: The president posted an image of a ‘federal victory note’ with himself on the front.

item.title: Container CEO Sees ‘Surprising’ Level of Demand in First Half
item.description: CEO of Hapag-Lloyd finds the level of demand for containers a ‘little bit surprising’

item.title: Why Modi Wants Indians To Stop Buying Gold
item.description: India has more than doubled import tariffs on gold just days after Prime Minister Narendra Modi asked citizens to stop buying the metal for at least a year.

item.title: HSBC’s Kettner Says Stock Rally Can Cope With Rising Bond Yields
item.description: Stocks can rally further as a powerful recovery in earnings and still-low positioning levels outweigh the threat from rising bond yields, according to Max Kettner, chief multi-asset strategist at HSBC Holdings Plc.

item.title: Gilts Become ‘Half-Hour Trade’ on Politics, HSBC’s Kettner Says
item.description: The UK’s fractious politics is making investors wary of long-term wagers in the country’s gilt markets, according to HSBC Holdings Plc’s Max Kettner.

item.title: Undersea Internet Cable Projects Are Getting Tangled in the Iran War
item.description: If construction ever resumes in the Persian Gulf, tech companies will have a new headache to deal with: Unexploded missiles and mines.

item.title: Equinor to Talk With Germany on Buying Oil From Costlier Wells
item.description: Norway’s biggest energy producer is to hold talks with major European consumers on whether they would support projects to recover costlier oil and gas supplies.

item.title: Jack Ma-Backed Ant’s Profit Fell 79% on AI, Health Care Costs
item.description: Ant Group Co.’s quarterly profit continued to decline after the company increased investment in artificial intelligence technology for its health care, large language model and payment services.

item.title: Equinox Gold Agrees to Buy Orla Mining in $5.1 Billion Deal
item.description: Equinox Gold Corp. agreed to acquire Orla Mining Ltd. in a cash-and-stock deal valuing the Canadian miner at about $5.1 billion.

item.title: Adani Power Seeks 80 Billion Rupees in Debt to Fund Expansion
item.description: Adani Power Ltd., India’s largest private thermal power producer, plans to raise 80 billion rupees ($836 million) through local debt markets this year to fund expansion, according to people familiar with the matter.
